generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance DS18S20TopTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision DS18S20 1-Wire digital thermometer positioned at the top layer of the Physical Twin (PT) thermal incubator, measuring temperature with ±0.5°C accuracy and 12-bit resolution (0.0625°C increments). Operates within -10°C to +85°C range and communicates via 1-Wire bus to central data acquisition system."
    DTDFVocab:producedData DS18S20TopTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance DS18S20MiddleTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision DS18S20 1-Wire digital thermometer positioned at the middle layer of the Physical Twin (PT) thermal incubator, measuring temperature with ±0.5°C accuracy and 12-bit resolution (0.0625°C increments). Operates within -10°C to +85°C range and communicates via 1-Wire bus to central data acquisition system."
    DTDFVocab:producedData DS18S20MiddleTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance DS18S20BottomTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision DS18S20 1-Wire digital thermometer positioned at the bottom layer of the Physical Twin (PT) thermal incubator, measuring temperature with ±0.5°C accuracy and 12-bit resolution (0.0625°C increments). Operates within -10°C to +85°C range and communicates via 1-Wire bus to central data acquisition system."
    DTDFVocab:producedData DS18S20BottomTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance DS18S20TopTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw temperature data transmission from DS18S20TopTemperatureSensor via 1-Wire bus to RaspberryPiDataAcquisitionNode, published asynchronously to RabbitMQTemperatureQueue with millisecond-scale latency."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom DS18S20TopTemperatureSensor
]

instance DS18S20MiddleTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw temperature data transmission from DS18S20MiddleTemperatureSensor via 1-Wire bus to RaspberryPiDataAcquisitionNode, published asynchronously to RabbitMQTemperatureQueue with millisecond-scale latency."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom DS18S20MiddleTemperatureSensor
]

instance DS18S20BottomTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw temperature data transmission from DS18S20BottomTemperatureSensor via 1-Wire bus to RaspberryPiDataAcquisitionNode, published asynchronously to RabbitMQTemperatureQueue with millisecond-scale latency."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom DS18S20BottomTemperatureSensor
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Resistive heating element with adjustable power output for maintaining thermal conditions within the Physical Twin (PT) thermal incubator. Operates within defined temperature range and communicates with PTController for setpoint adjustments and safety constraints enforcement."
]

instance ForcedAirFanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Forced-air distribution system with variable speed operation to homogenize heat within the Physical Twin (PT) thermal incubator. Implements aerodynamic control to maintain uniform temperature distribution and communicates with PTController for airflow regulation."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance ThermalIncubatorGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "CAD-based geometric representation of the Physical Twin (PT) thermal incubator including spatial dimensions, material properties, and structural components for structural and spatial analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithmEnabler
]

instance ThermalIncubatorBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based differential equation model representing dynamic thermal behaviors including heat transfer, thermal mass effects, and system responses to perturbations within the Physical Twin (PT) thermal incubator."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithmEnabler
]

instance ThermalIncubatorAnalyticalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Mathematical formulation for system performance analysis including fault detection, optimization, and predictive maintenance algorithms for the Physical Twin (PT) thermal incubator."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithmEnabler
]

instance RawTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated raw temperature readings from all DS18S20 sensors within the Physical Twin (PT) thermal incubator, including timestamped measurements from top, middle, and bottom layers."
    DTDFVocab:fromData DS18S20TemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StateEstimationEnabler
]

instance ProcessedTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Calibrated and filtered temperature data derived from raw sensor readings, accounting for environmental noise and sensor drift within the Physical Twin (PT) thermal incubator."
    DTDFVocab:fromData DS18S20TemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithmEnabler
]

instance DS18S20TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Aggregated data transmission combining temperature readings from all three DS18S20 sensors within the Physical Twin (PT), published to RabbitMQTemperatureQueue for distribution to calibration and monitoring algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom DS18S20TopTemperatureDataTransmitted, DS18S20MiddleTemperatureDataTransmitted, DS18S20BottomTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RaspberryPiDataAcquisitionNode : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Raspberry Pi 4 Model B embedded system serving as the central data acquisition node for the Physical Twin (PT), reading DS18S20 sensor data via 1-Wire bus and publishing to RabbitMQTemperatureQueue via Wi-Fi."
    DTDFVocab:enables TemperatureDataProcessingService, CalibrationAlgorithmService
]

instance RabbitMQTemperatureQueue : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "RabbitMQ message broker queue for asynchronous communication and message persistence, distributing temperature data from RaspberryPiDataAcquisitionNode to calibration and monitoring algorithms within the Digital Twin (DT)."
    DTDFVocab:enables StateEstimationService, FaultDetectionService
]

instance CalibrationAlgorithmEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Statistical and optimization-based calibration algorithms (e.g., least squares, Bayesian updating) that adjust model parameters (thermal conductivity, heat capacity) using real-time sensor data to maintain accuracy of the Digital Twin (DT) representations."
    DTDFVocab:enables ParameterCalibrationService
]

instance StateEstimationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Kalman filter-based state estimation algorithms that reconstruct the Physical Twin (PT)'s internal thermal state from noisy sensor measurements, reducing uncertainty in temperature distribution predictions."
    DTDFVocab:enables StateEstimationService
]

instance FaultDetectionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Anomaly detection algorithms analyzing sensor data streams to identify sensor drift, actuator failures, or environmental deviations by comparing observed PT behavior against simulated expectations."
    DTDFVocab:enables FaultDetectionService
]

instance PTController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Hardware/software interface (microcontroller or PLC) enabling bidirectional communication between Digital Twin (DT) and Physical Twin (PT), implementing safety constraints and executing actuator commands based on DT-generated insights."
    DTDFVocab:enables SupervisoryControlService, ClosedLoopFeedbackService
]

// C6: Services
instance TemperatureDataProcessingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time data acquisition service continuously collecting temperature sensor data from the Physical Twin (PT) via DS18S20 sensors and publishing to RabbitMQ for asynchronous distribution."
    DTDFVocab:provides RawTemperatureDataInsight
]

instance StateEstimationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic state estimation service reconstructing the Physical Twin (PT)'s internal thermal state from sensor measurements using Kalman filter algorithms, providing reduced uncertainty temperature profiles."
    DTDFVocab:provides ThermalStateInsight
]

instance ViolationDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Safety violation detection service monitoring Physical Twin (PT) temperature data against operational thresholds, triggering alerts for deviations beyond safe/optimal operating conditions."
    DTDFVocab:provides SafetyViolationInsight
]

instance CalibrationAlgorithmService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Parameter calibration service updating Digital Twin (DT) models using real-time sensor data to refine thermal parameters (conductivity, capacity) and maintain model accuracy with evolving Physical Twin (PT) conditions."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Fault detection service analyzing sensor data patterns to identify anomalies such as sensor drift, actuator malfunctions, or environmental deviations within the Physical Twin (PT)."
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDetectionInsight
]

instance ParameterCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Model parameter calibration service adjusting Digital Twin (DT) thermal models using statistical optimization techniques to minimize estimation errors between simulated and actual Physical Twin (PT) behavior."
    DTDFVocab:provides CalibrationResultInsight
]

instance SupervisoryControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Supervisory control service generating corrective action recommendations for Physical Twin (PT) operators based on Digital Twin (DT) insights, including actuator setpoint adjustments and safety interventions."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlRecommendationAction
]

instance ClosedLoopFeedbackService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Closed-loop feedback service implementing real-time adjustments to Physical Twin (PT) actuators based on Digital Twin (DT) generated insights, maintaining optimal operational conditions through dynamic control logic."
    DTDFVocab:provides ActuatorAdjustmentAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RawTemperatureDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Aggregated raw temperature readings from all DS18S20 sensors within the Physical Twin (PT) thermal incubator, serving as the foundational data input for all Digital Twin (DT) processing services."
]

instance ThermalStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Reconstructed internal thermal state of the Physical Twin (PT) including temperature gradients and distribution patterns, derived from state estimation algorithms applied to sensor data."
]

instance SafetyViolationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of Physical Twin (PT) operating conditions exceeding safe/optimal temperature thresholds, providing immediate alerts for potential equipment damage or operational inefficiencies."
]

instance ModelAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative assessment of Digital Twin (DT) model accuracy against Physical Twin (PT) behavior, including parameter estimation confidence intervals and calibration error metrics."
]

instance FaultDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identified anomalies within the Physical Twin (PT) system including sensor failures, actuator malfunctions, or environmental deviations detected through pattern recognition algorithms."
]

instance CalibrationResultInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Results of parameter calibration processes including adjusted thermal properties (conductivity, capacity) and performance metrics indicating model improvement after calibration."
]

instance ControlRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Recommended corrective actions for Physical Twin (PT) operators including actuator adjustments, operational parameter modifications, or maintenance interventions based on Digital Twin (DT) insights."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ActuatorAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustments to Physical Twin (PT) actuators (heatbed, fan) based on Digital Twin (DT) generated control signals to maintain optimal operating conditions."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C12: Environment
instance ThermalIncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Operational environment encompassing the Physical Twin (PT) thermal incubator including internal thermal dynamics, external ambient conditions, and interaction with Digital Twin (DT) systems."
    base:contains ThermalIncubatorPhysicalTwin
]

instance ThermalIncubatorPhysicalTwin : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical system representing the thermal incubator with embedded sensors (DS18S20), actuators (heatbed, fan), and controller interface enabling bidirectional communication with the Digital Twin (DT) for real-time monitoring and control."
    base:isContainedIn ThermalIncubatorEnvironment
]

// C13: Digital Twin
instance ThermalIncubatorDigitalTwin : DTDFVocab:DigitalTwin [
    base:desc "Digital representation of the Physical Twin (PT) thermal incubator integrating geometric, behavioral, and analytical models with real-time sensor data to enable predictive analysis, fault detection, and optimization services."
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment ThermalIncubatorEnvironment
    base:contains ThermalIncubatorGeometricModel, ThermalIncubatorBehavioralModel, ThermalIncubatorAnalyticalModel, RawTemperatureData, ProcessedTemperatureData
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin employs a **bang-bang controller logic** implemented on a **Raspberry Pi** to generate control commands for the Physical Twin’s actuators (e.g., fan and heatbed). Commands are transmitted via the same **RabbitMQ server** used for data acquisition, ensuring synchronized communication. A **safe protocol** is incorporated into the low-level driver to validate and authenticate commands before execution, preventing unsafe operations. Feedback from the PT is not explicitly described, but the system assumes real-time monitoring of actuator responses. Conflict resolution is not detailed, though the use of a centralized controller suggests hierarchical decision-making. Command execution confirmation is implied through the system’s monitoring and calibration feedback loop."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system operates within a **closed-loop temporal framework** where data collection from the Physical Twin (PT) is performed through periodic sensor readings, though explicit synchronization frequencies between the PT and Digital Twin (DT) components are not detailed in the provided documents. The system emphasizes **real-time calibration** of models via algorithms (e.g., state estimators, fault detectors) to update the cyber-physical system (CPS) and environmental models dynamically. While the document references a **physics-based constraint** (e.g., 'under 2 seconds' for heating capacity), this pertains to the PT's physical response time rather than explicit DT synchronization or model update intervals. No granular temporal specifications (e.g., latency requirements for monitoring or simulation services) are provided beyond the implicit need for low-latency feedback loops to enable safe and optimal operation, as implied by the closed-loop architecture. The temporal granularity is dictated by the PT's operational dynamics and the algorithms' ability to process sensor data in near-real-time for decision-making (e.g., safety checks or optimizations)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system follows a **modular architecture pattern** that integrates three core components: **Data** (collected from the Physical Twin via sensors), **Models** (abstract representations of the PT’s cyber and physical aspects), and **Algorithms** (e.g., fault detectors, supervisory controllers, state estimators, and optimizers). The system employs a **closed-loop orchestration mechanism**, where calibrated models inform **monitoring algorithms** that assess system safety and optimality. When violations are detected, the system enables **human-in-the-loop or automated interventions** to adjust the Physical Twin’s parameters or operations. Resource management is implied through the **dynamic updating of models** via calibration algorithms, ensuring computational efficiency and scalability. The DT framework adheres to a **service-oriented design**, where each component (data acquisition, model calibration, monitoring) operates as an interdependent module, coordinated to maintain alignment between the digital and physical domains. No explicit mention of **cloud-edge architectures**, **microservices**, or **orchestration frameworks** (e.g., Kubernetes, Apache Airflow) is provided, but the described workflow aligns with a **centralized, algorithm-driven constellation**."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
    The document outlines key aspects of the Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle implicitly through its description of the framework's components and integration with the Physical Twin (PT). While explicit lifecycle phases are not detailed, the following can be inferred:

    - **Development Phase**:
      - **Representation Types**: Models are initially abstract representations (e.g., CAD models for geometry, dynamic models for behavior) built to understand specific aspects of the system. These models are later integrated into the DT framework.
      - **Transition Management**: The document emphasizes that models used during development should not be discarded but **integrated into the DT** post-deployment, implying a transition from standalone modeling to a DT framework.
      - **Lifecycle Governance**: The DT framework is described as a **continuous integration of modeling, simulation, monitoring, and optimization**, suggesting iterative refinement and governance through real-time data and algorithmic updates (e.g., calibration algorithms for parameter estimation).

    - **Operational Phase**:
      - The DT operates by **collecting data from the PT via sensors**, updating models (e.g., calibration algorithms), and enabling **predictive simulations** to analyze current/emerging conditions.
      - **Monitoring and Control**: The DT includes **monitors for safety/optimality checks**, with human or algorithmic intervention for corrective actions (e.g., adjusting the PT based on violations detected).

    - **Evolution Phase**:
      - The document implies **dynamic evolution** through continuous data-driven updates (e.g., fault detection, state estimation) and optimization, though explicit versioning or governance frameworks are not mentioned.

    **Not explicitly detailed**: Phases like prototyping, validation, or decommissioning are not addressed.
  "
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The **fidelity and validity** of the Digital Twin (DT) are ensured through rigorous **model validation, verification, and uncertainty quantification**. Key aspects include:
- **Model fidelity requirements**: High fidelity is achieved by integrating **geometric, behavioral, and analytical models** that align with the PT’s physical and cyber characteristics. Fidelity is validated via **simulation reproducibility** under current operating conditions.
- **Validation and verification methods**: Models are validated against **real-world PT data** to ensure accuracy in predicting behavior. Verification involves **cross-checking simulations** with empirical observations (e.g., comparing predicted vs. actual system responses).
- **Uncertainty sources and quantification**: Sources of uncertainty include **sensor noise, environmental variability, and model simplifications**. These are quantified via statistical methods or **error propagation analysis** to assess model robustness.
- **Error detection and correction mechanisms**: The DT employs **calibration algorithms** to adjust model parameters dynamically, correcting discrepancies between simulated and observed PT behavior. **Fault detection algorithms** identify anomalies in real-time data, triggering corrective actions.
- **Quality assurance processes**: Models and data undergo **iterative refinement** through feedback loops, ensuring continuous improvement in fidelity. The DT framework enforces **safety and optimality checks** to validate system performance against operational constraints."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin’s technical network infrastructure consists of a **Raspberry Pi 4 Model B** hosting both the controller logic and the **RabbitMQ server**, connected to the Physical Twin via **Wi-Fi**. No explicit mention of network topology, bandwidth requirements, or latency specifications is provided. Reliability and redundancy mechanisms are not detailed, though the use of RabbitMQ’s persistent message queues implies fault tolerance for data transmission. Security protocols are not explicitly described beyond the low-level driver’s safe protocol for command validation."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system is deployed entirely on a **single-board computer (Raspberry Pi 4 Model B)**, with no mention of cloud, edge, or on-premise hosting platforms. Computational and storage requirements are not specified, but the system is designed for lightweight operation given its embedded deployment. Scalability mechanisms are not applicable, as the system is not described as distributed. Deployment models supported are limited to the standalone Raspberry Pi setup. Availability and reliability assurance are not detailed beyond the implied robustness of the RabbitMQ server and controller logic."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system incorporates a **safe protocol** embedded within the low-level driver to ensure the Physical Twin (PT) remains in a **safe operational state** at all times. The system employs **monitoring algorithms** to continuously assess whether the PT is performing **safely and optimally** by cross-referencing real-time sensor data with calibrated models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment. When deviations or violations are detected—such as unsafe operating conditions—the system triggers **corrective actions**, which may include human intervention or automated adjustments to maintain system integrity. The document explicitly highlights the integration of **calibration algorithms** to update models with the **best estimate of parameters**, ensuring the DT’s predictive and diagnostic capabilities remain aligned with the PT’s actual state. No explicit mention of authentication systems, threat mitigation strategies, or formal risk assessment methodologies is provided."
]
